title: Persistent Connections, Pipelining, Ordering, Reuse, and Closure · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs persistent connections, pipelining, ordering, reuse, and closure from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the persistent-connection request and response state machine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for persistent-connection request and response state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.
Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.
Preserve request-response ordering across pipelined HTTP/1.1 messages even when application work finishes out of order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Desynchronization Close
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 3926 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve HTTP/1.1 response order and reject reuse after incomplete or ambiguous input; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Close and discard connection state after ambiguous framing, parse failure, incomplete output, unexpected residual bytes, or state disagreement.

Inspect the Desynchronization Close trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Desynchronization Close.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for close and discard connection state after ambiguous framing, parse failure, incomplete output, unexpected residual bytes, or state disagreement. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Desynchronization Close, keep Desynchronization Close connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
